name: Low Gravity Zone
description: A volume or region reduces gravity effects for selected bodies, changing jump arcs, falling, and object motion.
design_notes: Low gravity zones should be authored as explicit trigger volumes with priority, blending, and affected body masks. Players need to read the boundary before crossing it, and systems such as jump tuning, projectiles, moving platforms, and AI navigation need to know which gravity field is active.
design_purpose: Create floatier traversal spaces without retuning the entire game's movement model.
Support puzzles where object weight, jump height, or projectile arcs change by region.
Give designers contrast between normal physics spaces and special environmental areas.
player_fantasy: The player feels momentarily light and can perform movements that normal gravity would not allow.

parameters.name: trigger_volume_id
parameters.description: Identifier for the zone that applies reduced gravity rules to entering bodies.

parameters.name: zone_gravity_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to the base gravity acceleration while a body is inside the zone.

parameters.name: entry_blend_ms
parameters.description: Blend window used when entering the zone to avoid sudden velocity discontinuities.

parameters.name: exit_blend_ms
parameters.description: Blend window used when restoring normal gravity after the body leaves the zone.

parameters.name: affected_body_mask
parameters.description: Body classes that should respect the zone instead of continuing with world gravity.

edge_cases: A player straddles the boundary with the character center outside but feet or projectiles inside.
Multiple zones overlap and disagree about priority, blend windows, or affected body classes.
A saved game loads with bodies inside the zone before trigger enter events have fired.
AI jump links or projectile prediction still assume normal gravity and choose invalid paths.
common_bugs: Gravity multiplier is applied cumulatively every frame instead of replacing or blending from the base field.
Leaving the zone snaps vertical velocity and causes sudden fall damage or ceiling collisions.
Projectiles and characters use different active zone ids, so aiming previews do not match actual arcs.
Trigger exit is missed when the zone unloads, leaving a persistent low-gravity state on the body.
balancing_notes: Lower multipliers widen jump and reaction windows but can make hazards too easy unless level scale adapts.
Boundaries should be placed where players can safely test the altered arc before facing punishment.
If projectiles are affected, combat difficulty may shift as cover, travel arcs, and dodge timing change.
accessibility_notes: Provide visual boundary cues and optional UI state so players know when altered gravity is active.
Reduce camera bob or auto-tilt in low gravity areas if floaty motion causes discomfort.

implementation_notes.unity: Use trigger colliders keyed by trigger_volume_id and maintain an active zone stack per physics_body_id. Apply zone_gravity_multiplier during the fixed movement integration path, blend entry and exit, and include active zone id in save and network snapshots.
implementation_notes.godot: Area2D or Area3D nodes can define gravity overrides, but custom controllers should still store active trigger_volume_id and multiplier explicitly. Debug draw zone boundaries and priority order so overlapping areas are understandable during tuning.
implementation_notes.unreal: Physics Volumes or Actor Components can apply per-character GravityScale changes. For multiplayer, the server should validate zone membership and replicate active gravity field state, while clients may predict entry blends for responsiveness.
implementation_notes.web: Represent low gravity as a region query in the fixed simulation step, not a render-only effect. Store active zone ids on bodies, clamp blends after tab resume, and keep projectile preview using the same zone lookup as simulation.

example_games.title: Super Mario Galaxy
example_games.note: Uses gravity changes and low-gravity-like spaces as traversal features.

example_games.title: Portal 2
example_games.note: Uses altered movement fields in puzzle spaces.
